title: Consultation History Entry
description: The canonical shape of a ConsultationHistory record — docs/29-PHASE-2A-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §4's already-locked sheet, and the concrete storage of docs/33-DOMAIN-MODEL.md §3.1's Timeline Event for this batch's one entry_type. Feeds the patient-facing Timeline (docs/29 §6) and Consultation History detail view (docs/29 §7). See consultation-history.md for the entry_type scope note, the record_id identity strategy (docs/40-CONSULTATION-IDENTITY-STRATEGY.md), and the source_ref sentinel-value convention.

record_id.description: The permanent identity of this consultation entry (docs/40 Q1/Q2). A UUID, generated once at creation, never reused or changed, and SQL-migration-safe by design (ADR-006). The only field the Timeline/Consultation History detail view should ever use to reference or fetch a specific entry — never row position (docs/40 Q4).

patient_id.description: The Patient Identity this entry belongs to (docs/33 §1.2, ADR-002). Every patient-facing fetch of this entry must verify this field matches the session-derived patient_id before returning it (docs/40 Q3, docs/39 §10) — record_id alone is not an authorization boundary.

entry_date.description: The clinically meaningful date the visit happened — not necessarily the same as created_at, since staff may enter or backfill an entry after the fact. This is the Timeline's sort key (descending), with created_at as an explicit tiebreaker for same-date entries (docs/39 §3).

entry_type.description: Every row this schema version stores is "consultation" — the enum is deliberately narrow to what ConsultationHistory actually holds today, not widened speculatively. docs/33 §3.1's more general Timeline Event model anticipates future entry_types (note/milestone) arriving via later entities/sheets (Care Plan, Digital Twin), not necessarily by widening this enum — a future batch should revisit this value set only once it actually needs to, per shared/README.md's versioning rule.

summary_text.description: The full, patient-facing entry text. Read-only for patients (docs/29 §7, docs/30 §2 "doctors decide") — no patient-editing endpoint exists or should exist for this field.

source_ref.description: Optional pointer to an already doctor-approved Phase 1.5 Consultation Summary record_id (docs/29 §7's second sourcing path), so its content isn't re-typed. Empty string when this entry was authored directly by staff with no linked Phase 1.5 record — the same legitimately-empty-until-set sentinel convention shared/schemas/login-token.schema.json's used_at already established, reused here rather than inventing a new null-handling rule.

created_by.description: Permanent — the staff identifier who created this record, an audit provenance field, never modified afterward.

created_at.description: Permanent — set once at creation, never modified afterward. Distinct from entry_date; see that field's own description.
